title: The Origami Crane
summary: A child describes learning to fold a paper crane in an art class at a school in Osaka.
body: Today my class learned to make an origami crane. Origami is the art of folding paper. Our teacher is Mrs. Tanaka. She is very kind and very patient. She gave each child one small square of pink paper. The paper was thin and soft. First, we folded the paper in two. Then we folded it again. After that, we made a long thin shape. I made many small mistakes. My paper was a little bit crumpled. Mrs. Tanaka helped me. She showed me each step slowly. After thirty minutes I finished my first crane. It was not perfect, but I was very proud. The wings were small and the head was a little crooked. My friend Hiro made a very tidy crane. He helped me with the last fold. We put all the cranes on a string near the window. They look like a small flock of pink birds. I want to make one hundred cranes. People say one hundred cranes bring good luck.

annotations.exampleEn: She loves origami and makes new shapes every week.
The crane is a tall bird with long legs.
My grandfather is very patient with small children.
She cut a small square of paper for the card.
He folded the paper in two and made a card.
Do not be afraid of small mistakes.
The note was crumpled in his pocket.
I am proud of my little brother.
The picture on the wall is a little crooked.
Her desk is always very tidy.
A flock of birds flew over the school.
Good luck with your exam tomorrow!

grammarNotes.title: Sequence words: first, then, after that
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể từng bước, ta dùng các từ chỉ trình tự: 'First, we folded the paper in two. Then we folded it again. After that, we made a long thin shape.' Những từ này giúp người nghe theo dõi từng bước theo đúng thứ tự.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When you tell steps, use sequence words: 'First, we folded the paper in two. Then we folded it again. After that, we made a long thin shape.' These words help the listener follow each step in order.

grammarNotes.title: Simple past for finished actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể chuyện đã hoàn thành, ta dùng quá khứ đơn: 'Our teacher gave each child one small square.' / 'I made many small mistakes.' / 'He helped me with the last fold.' Các động từ bất quy tắc thường gặp ở A1: gave, made, helped (đều dùng dạng quá khứ đơn).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use simple past for finished actions: 'Our teacher gave each child one small square.' / 'I made many small mistakes.' / 'He helped me with the last fold.' Common A1 irregular past forms: gave, made; regular: helped, showed.

translationVi: Hôm nay lớp tôi học cách gấp một con sếu giấy. Origami là nghệ thuật gấp giấy. Cô giáo của chúng tôi là cô Tanaka. Cô rất hiền và rất kiên nhẫn. Cô đưa cho mỗi bạn một miếng giấy vuông nhỏ màu hồng. Giấy mỏng và mềm. Đầu tiên, chúng tôi gấp tờ giấy làm đôi. Sau đó, chúng tôi gấp lại lần nữa. Tiếp theo, chúng tôi tạo thành một hình dài và mỏng. Tôi đã mắc nhiều lỗi nhỏ. Tờ giấy của tôi hơi nhăn. Cô Tanaka giúp tôi. Cô chỉ cho tôi từng bước thật chậm. Sau ba mươi phút, tôi gấp xong con sếu đầu tiên. Nó không hoàn hảo nhưng tôi rất tự hào. Cánh thì nhỏ và đầu thì hơi lệch. Bạn tôi Hiro gấp một con sếu rất ngay ngắn. Bạn ấy giúp tôi nếp gấp cuối cùng. Chúng tôi treo tất cả những con sếu lên một sợi dây gần cửa sổ. Chúng trông giống một đàn chim hồng nhỏ. Tôi muốn gấp một trăm con sếu. Người ta nói rằng một trăm con sếu mang lại may mắn.

questions.prompt: What did the class learn to make?
questions.explanation: 'Today my class learned to make an origami crane.'
'Origami is the art of folding paper.'

questions.prompt: What did the class do first?
questions.explanation: 'First, we folded the paper in two.'

questions.prompt: Why was the narrator's paper crumpled?
options: He or she made small mistakes
questions.explanation: 'I made many small mistakes. My paper was a little bit crumpled.'

questions.prompt: How long did it take to finish the first crane?
questions.explanation: 'After thirty minutes I finished my first crane.'

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel after finishing?
Who helped with the last fold?
questions.explanation: 'He helped me with the last fold.'

questions.prompt: Where did they put the cranes?
options: On a string near the window
questions.explanation: 'We put all the cranes on a string near the window.'

questions.prompt: What does 'tidy' mean in this text?
questions.explanation: Hiro's crane is described as 'very tidy', meaning it was done neatly.

questions.prompt: Why does the narrator want to make one hundred cranes?
options: To give them to the teacher
questions.explanation: 'People say one hundred cranes bring good luck.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says they made many mistakes but feel proud. Why are mistakes useful when you learn something new?
